title: veronica alpina subsp. pumila
intro: Veronica alpina subsp. pumila, also known as the alpine speedwell or alpine veronica, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Veronica alpina subsp. pumila include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using the plant for medicinal purposes.
